beats.activity: Wake up, make espresso, check weather and mood for the day.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; Saturday means no tower shift, but Tessa still wakes with the sunrise. Overcast and 64°F is cooler than yesterday's 77°—perfect for a low-key, reflective day. Espresso ritual sets the tone.

beats.activity: Leisurely breakfast and read poetry while sitting in the kitchen.
beats.reasoning: No work today; Tessa has time to linger with coffee and her poetry notes. Yesterday's energy was 50/100 (moderate), so a slow morning at home suits her mood and the overcast weather.

Mount Baker to Ballard is ~6 miles; budget 40 min for a Saturday morning drive. Farmers market is in full swing today, and Ballard is nearby—yesterday she was already in Ballard for board games at Mox, so the neighborhood feels familiar.

beats.activity: Browse farmers market, pick up fresh produce and local goods.
beats.reasoning: Tessa values simple, fresh food (made light dinner yesterday). Overcast weather won't deter her from an outdoor market. 80 min allows leisurely browsing and chat with vendors—fits her laid-back personality.

beats.activity: Drive to Fremont to visit the second farmers market.
beats.reasoning: Ballard to Fremont is ~2 miles but Saturday traffic and parking hunting can add time; budget 35 min. Tessa is unhurried and curious—visiting two markets in one morning is a leisurely Saturday ritual for someone who values exploration.

beats.activity: Explore Fremont farmers market and grab lunch at a vendor stall.
beats.reasoning: 70 min to browse and eat; Fremont has a quirky, artistic vibe that suits Tessa. Lunch at a vendor fits her casual style. Mariners game at T-Mobile Park is at 1pm downtown—Tessa doesn't appear to be a sports fan, so this world event doesn't pull her away.
Fremont to Mount Baker is ~4 miles; budget 40 min for midday traffic and parking. Tessa heads home mid-afternoon to rest and recharge before evening plans.

beats.activity: Unpack farmers market haul, cook a light meal, work on poetry revisions.
beats.reasoning: Back home by early afternoon. Tessa unpacks fresh produce, prepares a simple meal, and spends time on her poetry—yesterday she rewrote two stanzas, and she's still in that reflective groove. 2.5 hours allows unhurried kitchen and creative work.

beats.activity: Run or walk at Discovery Park for exercise and fresh air.
beats.reasoning: Mount Baker to Discovery Park is ~3 miles; budget 30 min drive + 20 min buffer. Overcast 64°F is ideal for a run/walk—not too hot. Tessa runs on stubbornness and espresso; a mid-afternoon outdoor activity fits her active, grounded personality. Clears the mind before evening.
Discovery Park back to Mount Baker is ~3 miles; budget 50 min to account for post-exercise shower and freshen-up time at home before evening.

beats.activity: Shower, rest, prepare dinner, and read or work on poetry.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home. Tessa recovers from the run, makes a simple dinner using farmers market ingredients, and enjoys quiet time. 1.75 hours gives her space to decompress before a potential social evening.

beats.activity: Drive to a friend's place in Capitol Hill for evening board games.
beats.reasoning: Mount Baker to Capitol Hill is ~3 miles; budget 40 min for evening traffic. Tessa values time with close friends and board game nights (she was at Mox Ballard last night). A Saturday evening social gathering fits her laid-back, connected personality.

beats.activity: Board games and conversation with close friends.
beats.reasoning: 3 hours and 50 min of social time; a relaxed evening with friends playing games and talking. Tessa values intimacy over crowds—a smaller gathering fits her better than the Mariners game or Pride crowds downtown.

Capitol Hill back to Mount Baker is ~3 miles; sleep beat implicitly includes the 30 min commute home and then 7.5 hours of rest (ending at 06:30 Sunday morning, well before the 06:00 deadline). Tessa wakes early by nature, so 06:30 is realistic for her Sunday morning.

diary: Woke early with the overcast sky and made my usual espresso—read some Mary Oliver in the kitchen, which felt like the right way to start a Saturday. Hit up the Ballard market first, grabbed some gorgeous heirloom tomatoes and fresh bread, then drove over to Fremont for round two. The energy there was different, looser somehow. Had a really good fish taco from one of the vendors and just let myself wander. Spent the afternoon prepping everything at home, tweaking some poems that have been sitting around half-finished. Discovery Park was exactly what I needed—the overcast made the water look slate-grey and calm. Finished the day with friends in Capitol Hill playing Ticket to Ride and drinking too much wine. Felt unglamorous and perfect.
thought_process: I think I needed today to be deliberately slow. The Mariners game was happening downtown but I steered clear—too many crowds, and I realized I was craving quiet instead. The farmers markets felt like the right move, something tactile and real after a week of managing airspace and runway conflicts. Poetry revisions have been nagging at me, so I carved out actual time for them instead of just thinking about them. The walk at Discovery Park shifted my energy; I'd been a little tense in my shoulders all morning. By the time evening came around, I was ready for people again. Friends, games, laughter. It's about balance—knowing when to retreat into myself and when to emerge.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
